description: Append content to the end of an existing note with a newline separator.
Archive a note, hiding it from active views and searches. The note is not deleted and can be restored later.

noteId.description: ID of the note to archive

description: Auto-archive stale, low-importance knowledge entries. Uses decay scoring (daysSinceUpdate / 90) and importance thresholds. Safety guard: never archives entries with importance >= 0.8. Default is dry-run mode — returns candidates without mutating.

maxDecayScore.description: Maximum decay score threshold — entries with decay >= this are candidates (default 0.9)

maxImportance.description: Only archive entries with importance below this (default 0.2)

dryRun.description: If true (default), returns candidates without archiving

description: Ask a question about your notes. Uses semantic search to find relevant note chunks, synthesizes an AI answer across multiple notes, and returns source citations with confidence scoring. Returns answer text, confidence (0-1), and per-source noteId, title, excerpt, and relevanceScore.

question.description: The question to ask about your notes

limit.description: Maximum number of source chunks to consider (default 5)

description: Archive multiple notes, removing them from default note listings without deleting them. Already-archived notes are silently skipped. All note IDs must belong to the authenticated user (all-or-nothing on auth failure). Maximum 100 notes per call.

Recall knowledge entries matching multiple tag patterns in one call. Use this when you need to load all entries for several tag categories at once (e.g. all architecture + pattern entries). Results are grouped by pattern, sorted by importance then freshness, and deduplicated across groups.

patterns.description: Tag patterns to match. Use "prefix/*" for prefix matching (e.g. "arch/*" matches "arch/database"), "prefix*" for starts-with matching, or exact tag names.

description: Move multiple notes to a target folder. Returns error if the folder does not exist or is not owned by the caller. All note IDs must belong to the authenticated user (all-or-nothing on auth failure). Maximum 100 notes per call.

folderId.description: Target folder ID to move notes into

description: Append tags to multiple notes at once. Existing tags on each note are preserved (no duplicates). All note IDs must belong to the authenticated user (all-or-nothing on auth failure). Maximum 100 notes per call.

tags.description: Tags to append to all specified notes

description: Consolidate related knowledge entries into a single new entry, archiving the originals. "merge" combines content verbatim; "summarize" uses AI to generate a coherent summary.

noteIds.description: IDs of knowledge entries to consolidate (2-20)

targetTitle.description: Title for the new consolidated entry

strategy.description: "merge" combines content verbatim; "summarize" uses AI to generate a summary

description: Fetch the most relevant knowledge chunks from notes within a token budget. Use this to load context for a task or question without exceeding your context window. Searches across both regular notes and knowledge entries using semantic similarity. Large notes are chunked (~500 tokens each) and only the most relevant chunks are returned. Returns chunks sorted by relevance, total tokens used, and whether the budget was exhausted.

query.description: Task description or question to find relevant context for

types.description: Filter by note type (e.g. ["knowledge", "note"]). Omit to search all types.

tags.description: Filter by tags — returns notes matching any of the provided tags

description: Create a directed edge between two graph nodes.
Create a graph node. Use nodeType "note" for note-linked nodes, "tag" for tags, or "concept" for free-form concepts.

label.description: Node label (e.g. note title, tag name, concept)

noteId.description: Link to an existing note by ID (for nodeType "note")

description: Create a new note.
Create a smart folder (saved search). The folder stores a search query and mode (fulltext or semantic) for quick re-use. Duplicate names in the same workspace are rejected.
Create a new workspace. A unique slug is auto-generated from the name. Pass a template to pre-populate with _project/ and _agents/ folders and starter notes.

template.description: Template to populate the workspace. Use "remedy-pod" (recommended) for the full Compound Development experience, or "default" for a minimal setup.

description: Get a summary of note-taking activity for a given day: notes created, notes updated, tags used, and total word count.

date.description: ISO date string (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to today.

description: Get or create a daily note for a given date (defaults to today). If a daily note already exists for that date, returns it. Otherwise creates one with a template. Daily notes are placed in a "Daily Notes" folder and tagged with "daily".

date.description: ISO date string (YYYY-MM-DD). Defaults to today.

description: Delete a graph edge.
Delete a graph node and all its edges.
Delete a note by ID.
Delete a smart folder (saved search) by ID.

id.description: ID of the smart folder to delete

description: Delete a workspace. For safety, you must confirm by providing the workspace name. The default workspace cannot be deleted. Notes and folders are moved to the default workspace.

confirmName.description: Workspace name for safety confirmation (must match exactly)

description: Extract named entities (person, project, concept, organization, location) from a note using AI. Creates graph nodes for each entity and "mentions" edges from the note to entities. Idempotent.

noteId.description: ID of the note to extract entities from

description: Find notes that are semantically similar to a given note using embedding cosine similarity. Useful for deduplication, merging related content, and knowledge graph cleanup. Requires embeddings to be generated.

noteId.description: Note ID to find duplicates for

limit.description: Maximum number of duplicates to return (default 5)

description: Find the shortest path between two nodes in the knowledge graph. Returns the path as an ordered list of nodes and edges.

fromNodeId.description: ID of the starting graph node

toNodeId.description: ID of the target graph node

maxDepth.description: Maximum search depth (1-3, default 3). Values above 3 are clamped.

description: Generate client-specific MCP configuration and CLAUDE.md / system prompt snippet for integrating m-notes into an AI coding agent. Returns ready-to-paste JSON config and markdown instructions.

Generate a Map of Content (MOC) — an AI-built index note that organizes related notes by topic. Creates a new MOC note or updates an existing one for the given folder or tag. Notes are ordered by semantic similarity and each gets a one-sentence AI description.

scope_type.description: Whether to generate MOC for a folder or a tag

scope_id.description: The folder ID (cuid) or tag value to generate MOC for

limit.description: Maximum number of notes to include in the MOC (default 50)

description: List notes that link to a given note via [[wikilinks]], with an excerpt around each mention.

noteId.description: Note ID to find backlinks for

description: Get semantic topic clusters for notes in the workspace. Returns cached cluster data with noteId, title, x/y coordinates, and clusterId for visualization. Returns null if no cached clusters exist — use the web UI to compute clusters first.
Return the note relationship graph: nodes and edges from [[wikilinks]].
Get knowledge base health stats for the active workspace: total notes, total tags, orphan count (no links), stale count (30+ days), conflict count, and embedding coverage percentage.
Retrieve cached knowledge conflict results from a previous scan. Each result includes a stale flag indicating if either note was updated after the scan.
Get neighboring nodes and edges within a given depth from a starting node.
Get a single note by ID including its full content.

Find a note by title (case-insensitive, partial match). Returns the full note when exactly one match is found. Returns a list of candidates (id, title) when multiple notes match. Returns an error when no match is found.

title.description: Title to search for (case-insensitive, partial match)

description: Get parsed YAML frontmatter from a note as a JSON object. Returns empty object {} if the note has no frontmatter.
Get outgoing wikilinks and backlinks for a note. Outgoing: notes referenced via [[Title]] in this note. Backlinks: notes that reference this note by title.

noteId.description: Note ID

description: Fetch multiple notes by ID, or paginate through all notes using a cursor. Pass `ids` (max 10) to fetch specific notes in order. Omit `ids` and use `cursor` to paginate through all notes (10 per page).

ids.description: Note IDs to fetch (max 10). Notes not found or not owned by caller are omitted.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor returned by a previous call. Only used when `ids` is omitted.

description: Retrieve the provenance chain for a note — the list of sources where its knowledge originated.

noteId.description: ID of the note to get provenance for

description: Get notes modified since a given timestamp. Returns notes sorted by updatedAt descending.

since.description: ISO 8601 datetime string — only notes updated after this time are returned

description: Get the full tool call replay for a specific session trace. Returns the complete toolCalls array with toolName, input, output, durationMs, and calledAt for each call.

sessionId.description: The session trace ID to replay

description: Retrieve a specific version's content.
Get rich context for a workspace: metadata, folder tree, recent notes, top tags, and pinned notes.
Show your current role and permissions in the active workspace

Get a high-level workspace overview: total notes and folders, nested folder tree with note counts, recent activity (last 5 modified notes), top 20 tags by usage, and note counts per type. Useful for AI agent orientation in an unfamiliar workspace.
Traverse the knowledge graph outward from a starting node up to N hops. Returns all reachable nodes and edges within the specified depth.

startNodeId.description: ID of the graph node to start traversal from

maxDepth.description: Maximum traversal depth (1-3, default 2). Values above 3 are clamped.

edgeTypes.description: Optional filter: only traverse edges of these types (e.g. ["wikilink","related"])

nodeTypes.description: Optional filter: only include nodes of these types in the result (e.g. ["note","concept"])

description: Find stale knowledge entries based on time-based decay scoring. Decay score = min(1.0, daysSinceUpdate / decayWindow). Score 0 = fresh, 1 = fully stale. Useful for prioritizing fresh information and flagging entries needing review.

threshold.description: Minimum decay score to include (default 0.5). 0 = all entries, 1 = only fully stale

decayWindow.description: Number of days for full decay (default 90)

tags.description: Filter to entries with these tags

maxImportance.description: Filter to entries with importance below this threshold (includes null)

description: Batch-import multiple knowledge entries in one call (max 50). Each entry is upserted by key — created if new, updated if the key already exists. All entries are validated before any writes; if any entry is invalid the entire batch is rejected.

key.description: Unique identifier for this knowledge entry

content.description: Markdown content of the knowledge entry

entries.description: Array of knowledge entries to ingest (1–50)

description: Create a typed relationship between two knowledge entries or notes. Supports 6 relationship types: supports, contradicts, extends, replaces, depends_on, related. Entries can be referenced by key (for knowledge entries) or noteId (for any note). If the same link already exists (same source + target + type), it updates rather than duplicates.

sourceKey.description: Key of the source knowledge entry (use this OR sourceNoteId)

sourceNoteId.description: Note ID of the source (use this OR sourceKey)

targetKey.description: Key of the target knowledge entry (use this OR targetNoteId)

targetNoteId.description: Note ID of the target (use this OR targetKey)

relationType.description: Type of relationship: supports, contradicts, extends, replaces, depends_on, related

description: Export all knowledge entries in a workspace as a structured snapshot. Use this to back up project knowledge, transfer it between workspaces, or review the full knowledge base at a glance. Returns entries sorted by importance (desc) then freshness (desc), capped at 200.

tags.description: Filter entries to those with at least one of these tags

format.description: Output format (default "json"). "markdown" returns a single human-readable document.

description: Store a structured knowledge entry (fact, decision, or context) that persists across sessions. Creates a new entry when the key is new, or updates the existing entry when the key already exists for the user+workspace. Knowledge entries are searchable via search_notes but hidden from the UI sidebar.

key.description: Unique identifier for this knowledge entry within the workspace (e.g. "project/database-choice")

content.description: Markdown content of the knowledge entry

confidence.description: Confidence score from 0.0 to 1.0 indicating how reliable this knowledge is

tags.description: Tags for categorizing and filtering this knowledge entry

description: List folders for the authenticated user with their hierarchy (parent-child relationships) and note counts. Supports cursor-based pagination.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor returned by a previous call

limit.description: Maximum number of results per page (default 50, max 100)

description: List notes for the authenticated user, optionally filtered by folder, note type, and/or workspace. Supports cursor-based pagination.

cursor.description: Pagination cursor returned by a previous call

limit.description: Maximum number of results per page (default 50, max 100)

description: List all object types (supertags) in the current or specified workspace. Returns id, name, icon, color, property schema, and note count.
List all pinned notes in a workspace.
List all prompt recipes for the authenticated user. Returns recipe id, name, and description.
List MCP agent session traces for the current user, in reverse-chronological order. Returns paginated summaries with id, label, timestamps, tool call count, and affected note IDs.

cursor.description: Cursor for pagination — pass the last session id from a previous page

description: List all smart folders (saved searches) in the workspace. Each smart folder stores a search query and mode (fulltext or semantic).
List all starred notes in a workspace.
List all tags in a workspace with note counts.
Extract markdown checkboxes (tasks) from notes. Returns each checkbox with its note, line number, text, and completion status. Filterable by status (open/done/all), tag, or specific noteId.

tag.description: Filter to notes with this tag

noteId.description: Filter to tasks from a specific note

limit.description: Maximum number of tasks to return (default 200)

description: List all members of the current team workspace with their roles
List notes in chronological order (newest first), optionally filtered by date range. Returns id, title, and createdAt for each note.
List version history for a note (id, createdAt, wordCount).

noteId.description: Note ID

description: List all workspaces for the authenticated user with note and folder counts.
Create, rename, or delete a folder. Actions: "create" (requires name, optional parentId), "rename" (requires id, name), "delete" (requires id, fails if folder contains notes).

id.description: Folder ID (required for rename and delete)

name.description: Folder name (required for create and rename)

description: Add or remove tags on a note. Tags are stored in YAML frontmatter. Adding an existing tag is a no-op. Removing a non-existent tag is a no-op. Returns the updated tag list after the operation.

Store or update a single fact/memory by key. Use this when the user says "remember this" or you need to persist a fact across sessions. Creates a new entry if the key is new, or updates the existing one. Returns whether the entry was created or updated, and the previous content if it was an update (so you can detect what changed).

content.description: The fact or memory to store

confidence.description: How confident you are in this fact, from 0.0 to 1.0

description: Change a folder's parent. Set parentId to null to move to root level. Cannot move the root folder. Maximum nesting depth is 1.

parentId.description: New parent folder ID, or null to move to root level

description: Generate a concise AI summary of a note by its ID. Works on both regular notes and knowledge entries. The note must have at least 100 words. Returns the summary text, note title, and word count.

noteId.description: The ID of the note to summarize

maxLength.description: Maximum length of the summary in tokens (default 150)

description: Find notes that have no tags and no incoming wikilinks — isolated notes with no connections. Useful for knowledge graph maintenance and discovering forgotten content.

limit.description: Maximum number of orphans to return (default 100)

description: Pin a note (max 10 pinned notes). No-op if already pinned.
Initialize the knowledge graph from existing notes, wikilinks, and tags. Idempotent — safe to run multiple times.

Load a merged project briefing in a single call — combines semantic knowledge recall, context chunks, and staleness info. Use this at the start of a session to get full project context without making multiple tool calls. By default returns an LLM-compressed compact briefing (~70% fewer tokens). Set full_context=true for raw structured JSON with all entries.

query.description: Natural language query describing what context you need

token_budget.description: Maximum tokens for knowledge + context combined (default 4000). 60% allocated to knowledge, 40% to context.

tags.description: Filter knowledge and context to entries with these tags

include_stale.description: Include stale knowledge entries as metadata (default false). These do not count against token budget.

full_context.description: Return full structured JSON with raw entries (default false). When false, returns a compact LLM-compressed briefing that uses ~70% fewer tokens.

description: Query notes by object type. Optionally filter by property values. Returns note id, title, objectTypeId, and properties.

objectTypeId.description: ID of the object type to filter by

propertyFilters.description: Optional key-value pairs to filter notes by property values (exact match)

description: Query the knowledge graph with filters. Returns matching nodes and their edges.

connectedTo.description: Node ID — return subgraph connected to this node

depth.description: Max traversal depth when using connectedTo

description: Get the connection subgraph around a note — all linked notes up to N hops via wikilinks and other edges. Takes a note ID directly (not a graph node ID).

noteId.description: Note ID to start from

description: Retrieve knowledge entries ranked by a weighted combination of semantic similarity, importance, and freshness. finalScore = semanticScore * 0.7 + importance * 0.2 + freshnessScore * 0.1. Null importance is treated as 0. Freshness decays linearly over the decay window (default 90 days).

query.description: Natural language query to search for

tags.description: Filter to entries with these tags

decayWindow.description: Number of days for full freshness decay (default 90)

description: Find notes semantically related to a given note. Returns notes connected via knowledge graph edges and/or embedding similarity, each with a similarity score. Use this to discover connections and build context around a topic.

noteId.description: The ID of the note to find related notes for

limit.description: Maximum number of related notes to return (default 5)

minSimilarity.description: Minimum semantic similarity threshold from 0.0 to 1.0 (default 0.7)

description: Restore a note to a previous version.

noteId.description: Note ID

description: Run a prompt recipe against a note. Fetches the note content, substitutes {{note_title}} and {{note_content}} placeholders in the recipe template, sends the prompt to the AI, and returns the response.

recipeId.description: ID of the recipe to run

noteId.description: ID of the note to run the recipe against

description: Save an AI conversation as a structured markdown note. Creates a note with formatted message history.

Trigger an async scan to detect contradictions between knowledge entries using LLM classification. Returns immediately with a scanId and status "scanning". Results are cached in the database and retrievable via get_knowledge_conflicts. Rate limited to 1 scan per 10 minutes per workspace.

similarityThreshold.description: Minimum cosine similarity for pairs to consider (default 0.8). Lower = more pairs, higher = stricter.

pairCap.description: Maximum number of pairs to evaluate (default 50, max 50).

tags.description: Filter knowledge entries to only those with these tags.

description: Find notes matching given tags. Use match="any" (default) to find notes with at least one of the tags, or match="all" to find notes that have every specified tag.

tags.description: Tags to search for (at least one required)

match.description: Match mode: "any" returns notes with at least one tag, "all" requires every tag

description: Search notes using full-text search, semantic (vector) search, or a hybrid blend. Returns matching notes with excerpts and relevance scores.

mode.description: Search mode: semantic (vector similarity), fulltext (PostgreSQL FTS), or hybrid (weighted blend)

limit.description: Maximum number of results to return

tags.description: Filter by tags (AND logic — notes must have ALL specified tags)

description: Resume a previous AI session by loading its context in one call — session metadata, decisions made, actions taken, tool call summary, and affected notes. Use this at the start of a follow-up session to pick up where a previous session left off.

sessionId.description: Session ID to resume. If omitted, uses the most recent session.

include_notes.description: Include affected notes with title, key, and excerpt (default true)

description: Log an AI conversation summary with decisions and actions. Creates an audit trail across sessions. If the same sessionId is used again, the new entry is appended to the existing log note (no duplicates). Session logs are searchable via semantic search and filterable by tags.

sessionId.description: Unique session identifier — same ID appends to the same log note

summary.description: Summary of what happened in this session segment

target.description: What was acted upon (file, feature, etc.)

description: Set the active workspace for this MCP session. Subsequent tool calls that don't specify a workspaceId will use this workspace. Pass either a workspace ID or a slug.

Set the importance score for a knowledge entry or note. Importance influences retrieval ranking in recall_knowledge — higher importance entries surface first. Score must be between 0.0 (low) and 1.0 (high). The note must belong to the authenticated user.

noteId.description: Note ID to update

description: Merge key-value pairs into a note's YAML frontmatter. Setting a field to null removes it. Non-frontmatter body content is preserved unchanged. If the "type" field is set, it is also synced to the database column.

fields.description: Key-value pairs to merge into frontmatter. Set a key to null to remove it.

description: Set or clear the object type (supertag) on a note. When setting a type, properties are initialized with defaults from the type schema. Pass objectTypeId as null or empty string to clear the type.

noteId.description: ID of the note

objectTypeId.description: ID of the object type to assign, or empty/omit to clear

description: Append a provenance entry to a note, recording where the knowledge originated. Each entry has a source type (url, mcp_tool, conversation, manual) and a reference string.

noteId.description: ID of the note to add provenance to

ref.description: Reference string (URL, tool name, conversation ID, or description)

description: Create a workspace, apply a template, and set it as the active workspace — all in one call. Returns a summary of what was created (workspace ID, folders, notes count).

template.description: Template to populate the workspace. "remedy-pod" (recommended) delivers the full Compound Development experience with 10 AI agents, communication protocol, and memory system.

description: Use AI to split a long note into 2-10 focused sub-notes. Returns proposed splits without creating them — call create_note separately for each split you want to keep. Returns { splits: Array<{ title, content }>, sourceNoteId }.

noteId.description: The ID of the note to split

description: Find notes that have not been updated in N days. Useful for knowledge maintenance, discovering forgotten content, and prompting review of outdated information.

days.description: Number of days since last update to consider stale (default 90)

limit.description: Maximum number of stale notes to return (default 100)

description: Suggest up to 5 relevant tags for a note based on semantic similarity. Finds the top-10 most similar notes and returns the most frequent tags across them, excluding tags the note already has. Generates an embedding on-the-fly if needed.

noteId.description: The ID of the note to suggest tags for

description: Get AI-powered tag and wikilink suggestions for a note based on semantic similarity. Uses the note's existing embedding to find similar notes (score >= 0.75) and suggests wikilinks to related notes and tags from those notes. Returns { wikilinks: Array<{ noteId, title, score }>, tags: string[] }.

noteId.description: The ID of the note to get suggestions for

description: Synthesize multiple notes into a single markdown document with wikilink citations. Accepts 2-20 note IDs, fetches their content, and generates a structured synthesis that references each source note using [[Title]] wikilinks. Returns { title, content, sourceNoteIds }. Does NOT auto-create a note.

noteIds.description: Array of note IDs to synthesize (2-20)

description: Toggle the starred status on a note.
Toggle a markdown checkbox in a note between done and undone. Specify the note and line number (from list_tasks output) and the desired state.

noteId.description: ID of the note containing the task

line.description: Line number of the checkbox (1-based, from list_tasks)

done.description: Set to true to mark done, false to mark undone

description: Unpin a note.

Update an existing note. You MUST pass the `version` field from your last read of the note. If another agent updated the note since you read it, the call will fail with a VERSION_CONFLICT error containing the current version. Re-read the note and retry.

version.description: Expected version from your last read of this note (required for conflict detection)

description: Update a workspace's name, description, or icon. Only provided fields are updated. If the name changes, a new unique slug is auto-generated.
Upload a file (image or PDF) to storage. Returns a markdown embed. If noteId is provided, the embed is appended to the note content.

noteId.description: If provided, append the embed to this note
